pattern: Each query reduces to counting prefixes of a tail of the array by product modulo k, which a segment tree of remainder histograms answers under point updates.
hints: After the update and prefix removal, you are counting prefixes of the remaining tail whose product leaves remainder x mod k.

Store in each segment a histogram of how many of its prefixes give each remainder, plus the product of the whole segment, so two segments merge by shifting the right child's histogram through the left child's product.
A point update rebuilds one leaf and its ancestors; a query merges the segments covering [start, n-1] and reads off the x-th histogram bucket.

edge_inputs.description: powers of two, k four, repeated update
start at last index leaves one element
update makes element divisible by k

stress.description: random arrays with random update-and-prefix queries
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 5)
    q = rng.randint(1, 40)
    queries = []
    for _ in range(q):
        idx = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        val = rng.randint(1, 1000000000)
        start = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        x = rng.randint(0, k - 1)
        queries.append([idx, val, start, x])
    return [nums, k, queries]
stress.note: indices and starts kept in range; updates persist across queries

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def resultArray(self, nums, k, queries):
        n = len(nums)
        size = 1
        while size < n:
            size *= 2
        # node: (hist list of length k, total product mod k)
        hist = [[0] * k for _ in range(2 * size)]
        prod = [1 % k] * (2 * size)
        def make_leaf(v):
            r = v % k
            h = [0] * k
            h[r] += 1
            return h, r
        def merge(lh, lp, rh, rp):
            h = list(lh)
            for rem in range(k):
                if rh[rem]:
                    h[(lp * rem) % k] += rh[rem]
            return h, (lp * rp) % k
        for i in range(n):
            h, p = make_leaf(nums[i])
            hist[size + i] = h
            prod[size + i] = p
        for i in range(n, size):
            hist[size + i] = [0] * k
            prod[size + i] = 1 % k
        for i in range(size - 1, 0, -1):
            h, p = merge(hist[2 * i], prod[2 * i], hist[2 * i + 1], prod[2 * i + 1])
            hist[i] = h
            prod[i] = p
        def update(pos, v):
            i = size + pos
            h, p = make_leaf(v)
            hist[i] = h
            prod[i] = p
            i //= 2
            while i >= 1:
                h, p = merge(hist[2 * i], prod[2 * i], hist[2 * i + 1], prod[2 * i + 1])
                hist[i] = h
                prod[i] = p
                i //= 2
        def query(start):
            # merge leaves covering [start, n-1] left to right
            lo = size + start
            hi = size + n - 1
            left_nodes = []
            right_nodes = []
            l = lo
            r = hi + 1
            while l < r:
                if l & 1:
                    left_nodes.append(l)
                    l += 1
                if r & 1:
                    r -= 1
                    right_nodes.append(r)
                l //= 2
                r //= 2
            ordered = left_nodes + right_nodes[::-1]
            acc_h = [0] * k
            acc_p = 1 % k
            first = True
            for node in ordered:
                if first:
                    acc_h = list(hist[node])
                    acc_p = prod[node]
                    first = False
                else:
                    acc_h, acc_p = merge(acc_h, acc_p, hist[node], prod[node])
            return acc_h
        ans = []
        for idx, val, start, x in queries:
            update(idx, val)
            h = query(start)
            ans.append(h[x])
        return ans
